title: microstegium fauriei var. geniculatum
intro: Microstegium fauriei var. geniculatum, also known as Japanese Stiltgrass, is a non-native invasive grass native to East Asia and introduced to the United States as an ornamental plant. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Microstegium fauriei var. geniculatum include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in traditional medicine.
